fs.writeFileSync(tmpPath, JSON.stringify(awInfo, null, 2));
console.log('Generated aw_info.json at:', tmpPath);

2026-03-02T09:22:00.8510746Z The PR has been created. Here's a summary of all the inconsistencies found and fixed across the `documentation/` folder:
2026-03-02T09:22:00.8511243Z 
2026-03-02T09:22:01.0830082Z ## Issues Fixed
2026-03-02T09:22:01.0830374Z 
2026-03-02T09:22:01.3028000Z ### 1. `Generate-code-from-TypeSpec.md` — Wrong pnpm package name
2026-03-02T09:22:01.5272209Z `npm install -g @pnpm` → `npm install -g pnpm`. There is no `@pnpm` scoped package on npm.
2026-03-02T09:22:01.5272613Z 
2026-03-02T09:22:01.7591219Z ### 2. `Quickstart-on-how-to-write-tests.md` — Outdated Mocha/Chai test patterns
2026-03-02T09:22:02.1951744Z The repo migrated from Mocha + Chai to **Vitest**, but the doc still showed:
2026-03-02T09:22:02.4270854Z - `import { assert } from "chai"` → replaced with Vitest imports
2026-03-02T09:22:02.6471252Z - `import { Context } from "mocha"` → replaced with `VitestTestContext` from `@azure-tools/test-recorder`
2026-03-02T09:22:03.3431889Z - Mocha-style `beforeEach(async function (this: Context)` with `this.currentTest` → replaced with vitest arrow-function style `beforeEach(async (ctx) =>`
2026-03-02T09:22:03.5671693Z - `new Recorder(context.currentTest)` → `new Recorder(context)`
2026-03-02T09:22:03.5672127Z 
2026-03-02T09:22:03.7991681Z ### 3. `resolve-pnpm-lock-merge-conflict.md` — Missing `/` in shell command
2026-03-02T09:22:04.0232068Z The combined one-liner was missing a `/` path separator:
2026-03-02T09:22:04.6748231Z `` git add `git rev-parse --show-toplevel`pnpm-lock.yaml `` → `` git add `git rev-parse --show-toplevel`/pnpm-lock.yaml ``
2026-03-02T09:22:04.6749030Z 
2026-03-02T09:22:04.9271668Z ### 4. `using-azure-identity.md` — Two stale descriptions of `@azure/identity` credentials
2026-03-02T09:22:05.6031000Z - **`DefaultAzureCredential`**: listed only 2 credentials in its chain; the actual implementation chains 7 (added `WorkloadIdentityCredential`, `VisualStudioCodeCredential`, `AzureCliCredential`, `AzurePowerShellCredential`, `AzureDeveloperCliCredential`)
2026-03-02T09:22:06.4957555Z - **`InteractiveBrowserCredential`**: described as using the deprecated "implicit grant flow" (it now uses **Authorization Code Flow with PKCE**); also removed a stale note claiming Node.js support was not yet available
2026-03-02T09:22:06.4958778Z

The correct package name is `pnpm`.\n\n---\n\n### `documentation/Quickstart-on-how-to-write-tests.md`\n\n**Fix**: Updated code examples from outdated Mocha/Chai test patterns to the current Vitest patterns used throughout the repository.\n\nThe repository migrated from Mocha + Chai to Vitest. All current SDK test files use `import { describe, it, assert, beforeEach, afterEach } from \"vitest\"` and `VitestTestContext` from `@azure-tools/test-recorder`. The documentation examples were still showing the old patterns.\n\nChanges in `glossary.spec.ts` example:\n- `import { assert } from \"chai\"` → `import { describe, it, assert, beforeEach, afterEach } from \"vitest\"`\n- `beforeEach(async function () { createRecorder(this) }` → `beforeEach(async (ctx) =\u003e { createRecorder(ctx) }`\n- `afterEach(async function ()` → `afterEach(async () =\u003e`\n\nChanges in `utils/recordedClient.ts` example:\n- `import { Context } from \"mocha\"` → `import type { VitestTestContext } from \"@azure-tools/test-recorder\"`\n- `createRecorder(context: Context)` → `createRecorder(context: VitestTestContext)`\n- `new Recorder(context.currentTest)` → `new Recorder(context)`\n\nChanges in `monitor.spec.ts` example:\n- `import { assert } from \"chai\"` + `import { Context } from \"mocha\"` → `import { describe, it, assert, beforeEach, afterEach } from \"vitest\"`\n- `beforeEach(async function (this: Context) { new Recorder(this.currentTest) }` → `beforeEach(async (ctx) =\u003e { new Recorder(ctx) }`\n- `afterEach(async function ()` + `it(..., async function ()` → arrow function forms\n\n---\n\n### `documentation/resolve-pnpm-lock-merge-conflict.md`\n\n**Fix**: Added missing `/` path separator in the combined git command at the bottom of the file.\n\n- **Before**: `` git add `git rev-parse --show-toplevel`pnpm-lock.yaml ``\n- **After**: `` git add `git rev-parse --show-toplevel`/pnpm-lock.yaml ``\n\nWithout the `/`, the command would fail because it would concatenate the repo root path directly with `pnpm-lock.yaml` without a separator.\n\n---\n\n### `documentation/using-azure-identity.md`\n\n**Fix 1**: Updated `DefaultAzureCredential` credential chain list.\n\nThe documentation stated only 2 credentials in the chain (`EnvironmentCredential` and `ManagedIdentityCredential`), but the current implementation in `sdk/identity/identity/src/credentials/defaultAzureCredential.ts` chains 7 credential types:\n- `EnvironmentCredential`\n- `WorkloadIdentityCredential` *(newly added)*\n- `ManagedIdentityCredential`\n- `VisualStudioCodeCredential` *(newly added)*\n- `AzureCliCredential` *(newly added)*\n- `AzurePowerShellCredential` *(newly added)*\n- `AzureDeveloperCliCredential` *(newly added)*\n\n**Fix 2**: Updated `InteractiveBrowserCredential` description from the outdated \"implicit grant flow\" to the current \"Authorization Code Flow with PKCE\".\n\nThe implicit grant flow is deprecated and the current implementation uses Authorization Code Flow with PKCE (as documented in the source code: `sdk/identity/identity/src/credentials/interactiveBrowserCredential.ts`). Also:\n- Removed the outdated configuration instructions for \"Implicit grant\" checkboxes (no longer needed with auth code flow)\n- Updated to note that the credential supports both Node.js and browser environments (was incorrectly stated as browser-only)\n- Removed a stale NOTE claiming Node.js support was a future item (it has been supported for a long time)","branch":"docs/fix-documentation-inconsistencies","title":"Fix documentation inconsistencies in documentation/ folder"},"name":"create_pull_request"}}}
